description: Universal Quick Capture (user requirement 2). A single, always-accessible command-style capture input the user can open from ANYWHERE in the dashboard to dump a task or thought in ~2 seconds. The user types naturally (e.g. 'call dentist tomorrow 3pm', 'buy groceries') and the existing braindump backend turns it into the right thing (task or event) with natural date parsing. Opened by the TopBar '+' button and by pressing 'n' when not typing in an input. Confirms with a subtle toast (e.g. "Added 'call dentist'"). Must feel instant and frictionless. No backend changes: reuse parseBraindump.
findings: Implemented Universal Quick Capture. New file frontend/components/layout/QuickCapture.tsx: a 'use client' centered modal (matte theme, framer-motion spring stiffness 400/damping 30) with an auto-focused single-line input and an Add button. Opens via the 'chronai-open-quick-capture' window CustomEvent (dispatched by TopBar) and via the 'n' keyboard shortcut. The 'n' handler ignores INPUT/TEXTAREA/SELECT/contentEditable targets (checks both e.target and document.activeElement) and ignores meta/ctrl/alt modifiers so it never conflicts with the Cmd+K command palette; preventDefault only fires when actually opening. Escape and backdrop click close the modal. On Enter/Add it reads accessToken from useSession() ((session as any).accessToken) and calls parseBraindump(accessToken, text) from lib/api-extended.ts (no backend change). Input is disabled while submitting to prevent double-submit. On success (counts > 0) it shows a subtle toast "Added '<title|summary|name|input>'" via useAI().addNotification({id:`qc-...`, text, type:'info', actions:[], timestamp, dismissed:false}) and closes; if nothing was created it shows a gentle 'warning' toast and stays open; on thrown error it shows a 'warning' toast and stays open so the user can retry. QuickCapture is mounted inside the AIContextProvider block in frontend/app/dashboard/layout.tsx next to <AIToast /> so addNotification works. TopBar.tsx: the existing '+' dropdown now has a 'Quick Capture' entry at the top (with an 'n' kbd hint) that dispatches the CustomEvent; all four original quick actions (Add Task, Add Event, Start Pomodoro, Start Focus) remain in the dropdown below a divider. Note: api-extended.ts BrainDumpEvent uses 'summary' (not 'title') and BrainDumpResult.counts is {tasks,events,habits} - handled accordingly. Added frontend/__tests__/components/QuickCapture.test.tsx (5 tests: 'n' opens on body, CustomEvent opens, submit calls parseBraindump with entered text + toast + close, 'n' guarded while focus in input, 'n'+meta ignored); mocks next-auth useSession, lib/api-extended parseBraindump, and useAI. Verification: `npm run build`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` (9 tests across 3 suites) all clean.
steps: Create frontend/components/layout/QuickCapture.tsx ('use client'). A centered modal overlay (matte theme: backdrop bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm, panel bg-[var(--surface)] border border-[var(--border)] rounded-2xl shadow-2xl) modeled on the visual style of components/CommandPalette.tsx and AIToast.tsx. Single-line text input (auto-focused on open) with a calm placeholder like "Capture a task or event... e.g. call dentist tomorrow 3pm". Use framer-motion enter/exit (spring stiffness ~400 damping ~30, ~200-300ms) consistent with CommandPalette.

Wire submission: on Enter (non-empty, not while submitting), read accessToken from useSession() ((session as any).accessToken), call parseBraindump(accessToken, text) from lib/api-extended.ts. Show an inline loading/disabled state on the input/submit while awaiting so it feels instant but not double-submittable. On success, close the modal and clear the input.

Confirm with a subtle toast via useAI().addNotification(...): build an AISuggestion-shaped object { id: `qc-${Date.now()}-${random}`, text, type:'info', actions:[], timestamp:Date.now(), dismissed:false }. Compose text from the result: if counts.tasks/events/habits indicate what was created, prefer naming the item, e.g. "Added '"+ (result.tasks[0]?.title || result.events[0]?.summary || trimmedInput) +"'". If nothing was created (all counts 0) show a gentle message like "Couldn't capture that - try adding a time or more detail." Never throw to the user; wrap the call in try/catch and on error show an 'info'/'warning' toast and keep the modal open so they can retry.

QuickCapture MUST be mounted inside the AIContextProvider subtree (it depends on useAI). In frontend/app/dashboard/layout.tsx, render <QuickCapture /> inside the AIContextProvider block (near <AIToast />), NOT inside AppShell/TopBar (TopBar is outside the provider and cannot use useAI).

Open/close bridging via window CustomEvents (same pattern as existing 'chronai-open-chat' / 'chronai-start-focus'): QuickCapture listens for a 'chronai-open-quick-capture' event to open. In frontend/components/layout/TopBar.tsx, change the '+' button so it dispatches `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('chronai-open-quick-capture'))` to open Quick Capture. Keep the existing quick-action shortcuts (Add Task, Add Event, Start Pomodoro, Start Focus) accessible - simplest approach: keep the existing dropdown but add a 'Quick Capture' entry at the top, OR make the primary '+' click open Quick Capture and move the four shortcuts into Quick Capture as secondary buttons. Either way, do NOT remove any of the four existing actions.

Add the 'n' keyboard shortcut inside QuickCapture.tsx: a document keydown listener that opens the modal when key === 'n' (and no modifier keys) AND the event target is not an editable field. Guard against inputs: ignore when document.activeElement / e.target is INPUT, TEXTAREA, SELECT, or has isContentEditable true. Call e.preventDefault() only when actually opening. Pressing Escape (and clicking the backdrop) closes the modal. Clean up the listener on unmount.
Ensure no conflict with the existing Cmd+K command palette: 'n' must NOT fire while typing, and Cmd/Ctrl combos must be ignored by the QuickCapture handler.

Add a jest test frontend/__tests__/components/QuickCapture.test.tsx following the existing test setup (jest.config.js, jest.setup.js, @testing-library/react). Mock next-auth/react useSession to return an accessToken, mock '@/lib/api-extended' parseBraindump, and mock useAI/addNotification (or wrap in AIContextProvider). Cover: (a) pressing 'n' on document.body opens the capture input; (b) typing text + submitting calls parseBraindump with the entered text; (c) 'n' does NOT open while focus is in an input/textarea. Keep mocks light so the test exercises the real component logic (open/submit/guard), not just static values.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking the TopBar '+' opens the Quick Capture command input from any dashboard page.
Pressing 'n' while not focused in an input/textarea/contenteditable opens Quick Capture; pressing 'n' while typing in a field does NOT open it.
Submitting natural-language text calls parseBraindump (reusing the existing backend) and, on success, closes the modal and shows a subtle toast naming what was added.
Errors and empty results are handled gracefully with a toast and never crash the UI.
All four existing TopBar quick actions (Add Task, Add Event, Start Pomodoro, Start Focus) remain accessible.
QuickCapture is mounted inside AIContextProvider so addNotification works.
New jest test passes and meaningfully exercises open/submit/input-guard behavior.
`npm run build`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` are clean.
